evals.prompt: How do I safely validate user input at a TypeScript API endpoint?
evals.expected_output: Use Zod at the API boundary with safeParse(). Define a schema with z.object(), call safeParse(req.body), and return a 400 with result.error.flatten().fieldErrors if validation fails. Use safeParse (not parse) so you control the error response rather than letting a thrown exception propagate. The result.data is fully typed after the check.

evals.prompt: Where should I store a JWT after login in a TypeScript web app?
evals.expected_output: Store JWTs in an HttpOnly Secure SameSite=Strict cookie — never in localStorage or sessionStorage, which are accessible to XSS. Set maxAge to a short expiry (15 minutes) and implement refresh token rotation for longer sessions. Use RS256 (asymmetric) rather than HS256 so you can verify tokens without sharing the signing secret.

evals.prompt: How do I prevent command injection when running shell commands in TypeScript?
evals.expected_output: Use execFileSync (or execFile/spawn) with a separate args array instead of a shell command string. execFileSync('git', ['clone', repoUrl]) — the array form never invokes a shell, so metacharacters in repoUrl are inert. Never interpolate user input into execSync template strings: execSync(`git clone ${repoUrl}`) is vulnerable to shell injection.
